- Certaines syntaxes ne sont pas du Mark: - Some syntaxes are not pure Markdown, but syntaxes specific to Discord
- Discord permet aussi un **sous-texte**: - Discord also allows **subtext** with `-#` at the start of the line
- Discord permet d’afficher une heure ou: - Discord allows you to display a time or date automatically adapted to each user's time zone
- Elles sont très utiles pour les salons: - They are very useful for rooms, roles, users, custom emojis, orders and dates
- Il faut utiliser un timestamp Unix en: - You must use a Unix timestamp in **seconds**
- La mise en forme s’effectue en plaçant: - Formatting is done by placing certain characters before and after the text
- Le texte dans un bloc de code garde so: - Text in a code block retains its raw display
- Les exemples de mise en forme affichés: - The formatting examples shown here are mainly there to show the syntax to use
- Les syntaxes avec `ID` ne fonctionnero: - Syntaxes with `ID` will only work correctly if you put real Discord IDs
- Les timestamps fonctionneront avec un: - Timestamps will work with a real Unix timestamp in seconds
- Pour citer plusieurs lignes dans un se: - To quote multiple lines in a single block, put `>>>` followed by a space at the start of the first line
- Pour citer une seule ligne, mettez `>`: - To quote a single line, put `>` followed by a space at the start of the line
- Pour créer un en-tête, placez `#`, `##: - To create a header, place `#`, `##` or `###` followed by a space at the start of the line
- Pour indenter une liste, ajoutez **2 e: - To indent a list, add **2 spaces** before the bullet or number
- Pour intégrer un lien dans un texte, m: - To embed a link in a text, put the text between `[]` then the link between `()`
- Pour masquer un mot, une phrase ou un: - To hide a word, sentence or paragraph, use `||` before and after the text
- Si vous voulez afficher les symboles s: - If you want to display symbols without Discord applying formatting, you can use `\` before the character
- Très utile pour écrire des exemples sa: - Very useful for writing examples without activating them
- Utilisez trois accents graves ``` avan: - Use three grave accents ``` before and after text to make a multi-line code block
- Utilisez un seul accent grave `` ` ``: - Use a single grave accent `` ` `` before and after text to make code inline
- Vous pouvez aussi choisir un format pr: - You can also choose a specific format with `<t:TIMESTAMP:STYLE>`
- Vous pouvez aussi faire des listes num: - You can also make numbered lists with `1.` followed by a space
- Vous pouvez faire des listes avec `-`: - You can make lists with `-` or `*` followed by a space
- Vous pouvez utiliser `*`, `_`, `~` sel: - You can use `*`, `_`, `~` depending on the desired effect

Mode sans vidéo: vous pouvez noter, calc: No-video mode: you can score, calculate and export the results. You can leave this area empty and then create/edit the lines directly in the spreadsheet.

No-video mode: you can score, calculate and export the results. Video playback, MediaInfo, and player-related frame/timecode functions will be unavailable until the files are imported. To automatically link later, use the same name format (ex: Participant - Clip Name). You can leave this area empty and then create/edit the lines directly in the spreadsheet.

No-video mode: you can score, calculate and export results. Video playback, MediaInfo and frame/timecode features tied to the player stay unavailable until files are imported. To link entries automatically later, use the same naming format (example: Pseudonym - Clip name). You can also leave this area empty and create or edit rows directly in the spreadsheet afterward.
